title: Absolute Pot-fection
description: A "What Could Have Been" of minecraft Pottery. Improving textures while adding depth and variety with 20+ new Sherds to boot.
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/iAvkjHIxhzg" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Welcome to **ABSOLUTE POT-FECTION**. The *absolute* expansion of the vanilla Minecraft pottery system, inspired by the original 1.17 pottery system that was never fully developed. Both Decorated Pots and Flower Pots have been updated through this experience.

## The Pottery System

Decorated Pots and Flower Pots are crafted in the same way you always made them, with **Clay Balls** in place of Bricks to give the **Unfired** block variant. *Now forget the crafting table, pottery is a hands-on experience.* To alter Pots you must place them into the world and interact with them using a variety of items.

- **Pottery Sherd** - **Right-click** an Unfired Decorated Pot to stamp the sherd's texture onto the side you are facing. The sherd is **not consumed**, allowing for unlimited pots in even the most limited world.
- **Brush** - **Right-click** an Unfired Decorated Pot to **clear** the design from that face.
- **Dyes** - **Right-click** an Unfired Pot with any of the 16 vanilla dyes to **apply color**. Applying a new dye instantly overwrites the current color. *This consumes the dye.*
- **Water Bottle** - **Right-click** to **wash off** all dye, returning the pot to its natural clay color. *This consumes the water.*

### Firing

To finalize a design, you must **Fire** the Pot. Place the Unfired block above an open flame or a lit Campfire. **Flower Pots** fire in **3 seconds**. **Decorated Pots** fire in **6 seconds**.

Once fired, Pots behave exactly as they do in Vanilla Minecraft. Flower Pots showcase flora, and Decorated Pots store up to a stack of items.

### World Generation
The Decorated and Flower Pots found in **Trial Chambers** have been updated to use this new system seamlessly. However no new pottery sherds are available to find, though this is subject to change in future updates.
Absolute Pot-Fection does not make any changes to Archaeological structures.

## 21 New Pottery Sherds
All 21 new Pottery Sherds are crafted by combining a **Brick** with another item in a crafting grid or the player inventory.

| Item Used | Sherd Title |
| :--- | :--- |
| Netherite Scrap | *Challenge* |
| Firework (Down) | *Fall* |
| Enchanted Golden Apple | *Family* |
| Wind Charge | *Float* |
| Obsidian | *Gateway* |
| Leather | *Hold* |
| Totem of Undying | *Life* |
| Phantom Membrane | *Lunar* |
| Note Block | *Melody* |
| Firework (Top Left) | *Path* |
| Redstone | *Power* |
| Prismarine Shard | *Protector* |
| Firework (Up) | *Rise* |
| Flint | *Shatter* |
| Gold Ingot | *Snout* |
| Mangrove Propagule | *Sprout* |
| Resin Clump | *Stare* |
| Emerald | *Testificate* |
| Cactus | *Thorns* |
| Firework (Bottom Right) | *Trail* |
| Shulker Shell | *Visitor* |

Some of these sherds require very rare or unique items, such as Netherite Scrap, Wind Charges, Shulker Shells and the Totem of Undying. Having access to such rare patterns allows pots to display your **personal story**, where you've been (Netherite Scrap, Mangrove Propagule, Cactus) and what you've faced (Resin, Shulker Shell, Wind Charge).

## Frequently Asked Questions
### Why am I seeing a black cube instead of a pot?
Any mods that directly intefere with the vanilla core shaders prior to release version 26.2 will cause the pots to render where they shouldn't with a pitch-black texture. Unfortunately this is an incompatability issue that cannot be resolved by a Datapack. These mods would commonly include Optifine, Sodium and possibly Distant Horizons.


Have a question? Something not working right? Join the Discord to chat directly and report any bugs you find!
